flavorText: Evokers shape raw magical energy into devastating elemental and force effects.

features.description: The gold and time you must spend to copy an Evocation spell into your spellbook are halved.
When you cast an Evocation spell that affects other creatures you can see, you can protect a number of them equal to 1 + the spell's level from full effect.
Your damaging cantrips affect even creatures that succeed on their saving throws, taking half damage if they would normally take none.
Add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of any wizard Evocation spell you cast.
When casting a wizard spell of 5th level or lower that deals damage, you can deal maximum damage. Repeated uses before a long rest deal increasing necrotic damage to you.

evoker-evocation-savant.summary: Your spellbook growth rewards committing to destructive arcane force rather than dabbling.

evoker-sculpt-spells.summary: Shape area blasts around allies so the wizard can cast aggressively without wrecking the party line.
evoker-sculpt-spells.description: Sculpt Spells is one of the reasons Evoker works at a real table. It gives the subclass permission to use splash damage magic boldly instead of spending every round apologizing for friendly fire.

evoker-potent-cantrip.summary: Even your at-will damage magic remains relevant against targets that normally blunt save-based cantrips.

evoker-empowered-evocation.summary: Your Intelligence starts directly pushing destructive spell damage higher.

evoker-overchannel.summary: Force a lower-level damage spell to hit at maximum output when a huge spike matters more than your own comfort.
evoker-overchannel.description: Overchannel is the Evoker’s boss-fight lever. It trades safety for certainty and should feel like a dangerous choice to force a blast to peak efficiency.
